2604.09937.title: HealthAdminBench: Evaluating Computer-Use Agents on Healthcare Administration Tasks
2604.09937.paper_summary: Healthcare administration accounts for over $1 trillion in annual spending, making it a promising target for LLM-based computer-use agents (CUAs). While clinical applications of LLMs have received significant attention, no benchmark exists for evaluating CUAs on end-to-end administrative workflows. To address this gap, we introduce HealthAdminBench, a benchmark comprising four realistic GUI environments: an EHR, two payer portals, and a fax system, and 135 expert-defined tasks spanning three administrative task types: Prior Authorization, Appeals and Denials Management, and Durable Medical Equipment (DME) Order Processing. Each task is decomposed into fine-grained, verifiable subtasks, yielding 1,698 evaluation points. We evaluate seven agent configurations under multiple prompting and observation settings and find that, despite strong subtask performance, end-to-end reliability remains low: the best-performing agent (Claude Opus 4.6 CUA) achieves only 36.3 percent task success, while GPT-5.4 CUA attains the highest subtask success rate (82.8 percent). These results reveal a substantial gap between current agent capabilities and the demands of real-world administrative workflows. HealthAdminBench provides a rigorous foundation for evaluating progress toward safe and reliable automation of healthcare administrative workflows.
2604.09937.paper_summary_zh: 醫療管理每年支出超過1兆美元，使其成為基於LLM的計算機使用代理（CUA）的有前景目標。雖然LLM的臨床應用受到了廣泛關注，但目前尚無基準可用於評估CUA在端到端行政工作流程中的表現。為了解決這一空白，我們介紹了HealthAdminBench，這是一個基準，包含四個現實的GUI環境：一個電子健康紀錄（EHR）、兩個支付者門戶和一個傳真系統，以及135個專家定義的任務，涵蓋三種類型的行政任務：事前授權、上訴和拒絕管理，以及耐用醫療設備（DME）訂單處理。每個任務被分解為細粒度的、可驗證的子任務，總共產生1,698個評估點。我們在多種提示和觀察設置下評估了七種代理配置，發現儘管子任務的表現強勁，但端到端的可靠性仍然較低：表現最佳的代理（Claude Opus 4.6 CUA）僅達到36.3%的任務成功率，而GPT-5.4 CUA則實現了最高的子任務成功率（82.8%）。這些結果揭示了當前代理能力與現實世界行政工作流程需求之間的巨大差距。HealthAdminBench為評估朝著安全和可靠的醫療行政工作流程自動化的進展提供了一個嚴謹的基礎。
2604.09937.author: Suhana Bedi et.al.
2604.09937.authors: Suhana Bedi, Ryan Welch, Ethan Steinberg, Michael Wornow, Taeil Matthew Kim, Haroun Ahmed, Peter Sterling, Bravim Purohit, Qurat Akram, Angelic Acosta, Esther Nubla, Pritika Sharma, Michael A. Pfeffer, Sanmi Koyejo, Nigam H. Shah
